title: Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status
subtitle: Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.
caption:
Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status

Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.

The line chart tracks weekly mortality in Chile from October 2021 through March 2023, comparing four distinct vaccination cohorts: individuals with zero or one dose, two doses, three doses, and four doses. The vertical axis measures deaths per 100,000 people, ranging from 0 to 12, while the horizontal axis marks time. The data shows a clear stratification where higher vaccination counts correspond to lower death rates. The line representing zero or one dose sits consistently at the top of the chart, reaching a peak of nearly 12 deaths per 100,000 in early 2022. The line for two doses follows a similar trajectory but remains lower, peaking around 11 deaths per 100,000 during the same period. The three-dose line stays significantly flatter, rising only to approximately 4 deaths per 100,000 at the height of the wave. The four-dose line remains at the bottom, hovering near zero throughout the timeline. All groups show a synchronized spike in early 2022 followed by a decline, though the magnitude of this spike varies drastically by group. The mortality rate for the 'All ages' group is age-standardized to account for the different vaccination rates of older and younger people. The visual gap between the unvaccinated or partially vaccinated groups and the fully vaccinated groups remains wide across the entire period. The highest death rates occur among those with zero or one dose, while the lowest rates occur among those with four doses.
